analysis_metadata.specification: Matter R1.4, Section 11.26 Commissioner Control Cluster, Document 23-27349

property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: FSM explicitly checks CASE session, transitions to error state if absent
Client obligation, server enforces defensively. FSM correctly rejects unsupported categories.
property_verdicts.caveat: Client-side obligation cannot be verified in server FSM

property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: FSM returns FAILURE for duplicate RequestID and unsupported category, violating 'SHALL always return SUCCESS' requirement
All paths from Approval_Request_Accepted generate event, no path to skip event
FSM detects duplicates and returns FAILURE as per SHOULD requirement
FSM validates RequestID exists in stored approvals, rejects if not found
Deferred verification at commissioning time (Device_Attestation_Verification state)
property_verdicts.caveat: Verification deferred to commissioning, not at approval

property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: FSM validates NodeID and FabricID match stored approval, rejects on mismatch
Same as PROP_006, FSM validates RequestID existence
FSM checks CASE session for CommissionNode, fails with UNEXPECTED_ACCESS if absent
Only path to send response is through Approval_Granted state (SUCCESS event)
FSM tracks has_responded_with_window, rejects duplicate CommissionNode
Client-side timer modeled, responses invalid after ResponseTimeoutSeconds
Client validates device match before opening window, error state on mismatch
Server verifies VendorID/ProductID from Basic Information Cluster against approval
Mismatch leads to Commissioning_Aborted_With_Error, no path to completion
FSM calls indicate_error_to_user() on abort, SHOULD requirement implemented
Event fields copied directly from stored request, no transformation
Three distinct states for SUCCESS/FAILURE/TIMEOUT with correct StatusCode
Configuration property, FSM uses bitmap correctly, assumes correct setup
property_verdicts.caveat: Static configuration, not runtime FSM property

property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: Configuration property, FSM uses attribute correctly, assumes correct setup
property_verdicts.caveat: Static configuration, not runtime FSM property

property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: SHOULD requirement (advisory), FSM doesn't enforce (correctly). Security depends on PROP_008.
FSM has no approval expiration timer, approvals remain valid indefinitely
FSM sets FabricIndex correctly, isolation enforced by transport layer
property_verdicts.caveat: Transport layer responsibility, not application FSM

property_verdicts.verdict_explanation: FSM validates Iterations [1000,100000] and Salt length [16,32]
FSM validates Discriminator <= 4095
FSM state structure enforces sequence, no transition for out-of-order messages
RequestID binds approval to commission, no path to commission without approval
Single-state FSM ensures mutual exclusion, assumes proper cleanup
FSM models both paths (with/without user approval), MAY requirement satisfied

attack_path.violation: Returns FAILURE instead of SUCCESS for format-valid request

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: FSM performs semantic validation (duplicate detection, category check) before returning SUCCESS. Spec says 'SHALL always return SUCCESS' for correctly formatted requests.

specification_evidence.quote: The server SHALL always return SUCCESS to a correctly formatted RequestCommissioningApproval command, and then generate a CommissioningRequestResult event associated with the command's accessing fabric once the result is ready.
specification_evidence.context: Describes two-phase protocol: immediate SUCCESS response, then event with actual result
specification_evidence.supports_verdict: Spec explicitly requires 'always return SUCCESS' for correctly formatted requests

specification_evidence.quote: If the RequestID and client NodeID of a RequestCommissioningApproval match a previously received RequestCommissioningApproval and the server has not returned an error or completed commissioning of a device for the prior request, then the server SHOULD return FAILURE.
specification_evidence.context: Contradicts previous statement about 'always return SUCCESS'
specification_evidence.gap: Specification contains contradiction: 'always return SUCCESS' vs 'SHOULD return FAILURE for duplicates'

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: Server SHALL always return SUCCESS to correctly formatted request
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: Definition of 'correctly formatted' - syntactic vs semantic validation. When should duplicate/category checks occur - before or after SUCCESS response?
specification_gap.consequence: Implementation ambiguity: some implementations return FAILURE synchronously (FSM), others would return SUCCESS then FAILURE event
specification_gap.security_impact: Protocol confusion, client cannot distinguish format errors from semantic errors

steps: Client sends correctly formatted RequestCommissioningApproval (all fields valid, proper types)
Request has duplicate RequestID from previous pending request
Server detects duplicate in check_duplicate_request() guard
Client expects SUCCESS response + eventual CommissioningRequestResult event
Client waits for event that never arrives (because synchronous FAILURE was sent)
Result: Protocol deadlock, client in inconsistent state
counterexample_scenario.note: Not an attacker exploit but design flaw causing protocol ambiguity

recommendations: Spec: Clarify 'correctly formatted' means syntactic validity only (schema compliance)
Spec: State that duplicate/category checks occur in Phase 2 (event), not Phase 1 (response)
FSM: Change to return SUCCESS for all format-valid requests, generate FAILURE/TIMEOUT events for semantic errors
Alternative: Remove 'always return SUCCESS' requirement and document synchronous rejection cases

attack_path.guard: session_type == CASE AND identity_matches AND requestid_valid AND approval_success_confirmed AND NOT already_used
attack_path.violation: No time-based guard, approval valid indefinitely

fsm_analysis.violation_mechanism: FSM has no timer or expiration mechanism for Approval_Granted state. Approval remains valid indefinitely until used or server restart. No state transition on timeout.

specification_evidence.quote: Clients SHOULD send the CommissionNode command immediately upon receiving a CommissioningRequestResult event with StatusCode of SUCCESS
specification_evidence.source: Section 11.26.6.5, 'CommissionNode Command', Page 5 (1054), implied from immediate send recommendation
specification_evidence.context: Spec recommends immediate sending but doesn't mandate or enforce
specification_evidence.gap: Spec does NOT explicitly require approval expiration or define maximum validity period

specification_gap.what_spec_claims: Implicit assumption that approvals are used promptly (SHOULD send immediately)
specification_gap.what_spec_fails_to_specify: Explicit approval expiration requirement, maximum validity period, expiration mechanism (timer/event), what happens if CommissionNode arrives after long delay
specification_gap.consequence: Implementations vary: some may expire approvals, others (like this FSM) don't. Stale approvals can be exploited.
specification_gap.security_impact: Time-shifted attacks, stale user consent, commissioning at unexpected times

steps: Time T0: Victim requests approval for device (RequestID = R)
Time T0+1s: Victim receives CommissioningRequestResult with SUCCESS
Time T0+2s: Attacker observes/intercepts RequestID R (via traffic analysis or compromise)
Time T0 to T0+[WEEKS]: Victim doesn't send CommissionNode (delayed, forgot, interrupted)
FSM State: Approval_Granted (approval_valid == true, NO EXPIRATION)
Time T0+[WEEKS]: Attacker sends CommissionNode with stolen RequestID R
Server checks: CASE session ✓, RequestID valid ✓, Identity matches attacker ✗ (PROP_008 prevents this specific attack)
Alternative: Victim finally sends CommissionNode weeks later at unexpected time
Result: Commissioning happens with weeks-old approval, violating temporal security
counterexample_scenario.note: Attack mitigated by PROP_008 (identity binding) but time-shifted legitimate use still problematic

realistic_threat_scenarios.name: Device Reset Attack
realistic_threat_scenarios.description: User approves commissioning, device fails/resets, weeks later device autonomously retries using old approval

realistic_threat_scenarios.name: Abandoned Commissioning
realistic_threat_scenarios.description: User starts approval, gets interrupted/forgets, weeks later client auto-retries and succeeds unexpectedly

realistic_threat_scenarios.name: Stolen Credential + Time Delay
realistic_threat_scenarios.description: Attacker steals credentials, waits for user to forget pending approval, uses RequestID months later

recommendations: Spec: Add requirement: 'Server SHALL invalidate approval if CommissionNode not received within manufacturer-determined period (RECOMMENDED: 300 seconds)'
Spec: Add requirement: 'Server SHALL generate CommissioningRequestResult event with StatusCode TIMEOUT if approval expires before CommissionNode'
FSM: Add Approval_Expiring and Approval_Expired states
FSM: Add start_approval_timer() call in transition to Approval_Granted
FSM: Add Approval_Granted -> Approval_Expired transition with guard: time_since_approval >= approval_validity_period
FSM: Add time-based guard in CommissionNode acceptance: (current_time - approval_timestamp) <= validity_period

summary.next_action: Analysis complete. Recommend fixing PROP_003 and PROP_023 violations.
